authors: De Carolis G, Paroli M, Tollapi L, Doust M, Burgher A H, Yu C, Yang T, Morgan D, Amirdelfan K, Kapural L, Sitzman B T, Bundschu R, Vallejo R, Benyamin R, Yearwood T L, Gliner B E, Powell A A, Bradley K
title: Paresthesia-Independence: An Assessment of Technical Factors Related to 10 kHz Paresthesia-Free Spinal Cord Stimulation
study_question: Is HF10 therapy pain relief related to paresthesia-pain overlap or mediolateral paresthesia distribution?
population_assessed: Patients with chronic back and leg pain implanted with HF10 SCS device.
outcome_measures: Pain relief, paresthesia-pain overlap, mediolateral paresthesia distribution
inclusion_criteria: HF10 SCS implanted patients with chronic back and leg pain.
answer_to_study_question: No correlation between paresthesia overlap/position and HF10 therapy efficacy was found.
